title: centaurea rupestris subsp. ceratophylla
intro: Centaurea rupestris subsp. ceratophylla, also known as the Ceratophyllum or mountain centaury, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Traditionally, Centaurea rupestris subsp. ceratophylla is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
